question: What is considered the first Puerto Rican feature film?
fact: Romance tropical (1934), directed by Juan Emilio Viqueira, is the earliest surviving Puerto Rican feature film.

question: Which Puerto Rican actor won an Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor in 1962?
fact: Rita Moreno won for West Side Story — she also won a Grammy, Tony and Emmy, completing the EGOT.

question: Raúl Juliá, born in San Juan, is beloved for playing which iconic character in two early-1990s Hollywood films?
fact: Juliá played Gomez Addams in The Addams Family (1991) and Addams Family Values (1993). He earned four Tony nominations on Broadway before his film career peaked.

question: Benicio del Toro won the Cannes Best Actor award for which film?
options: Fear and Loathing in Las Vegas
fact: Del Toro won at Cannes 2000 for Traffic, then the Oscar for Best Supporting Actor the same year.

question: Which production company, founded in Puerto Rico, produced 'Los peloteros' (1953)?
fact: DIVEDCO (Division of Community Education) produced social and cultural films from the 1940s–70s.

question: José Ferrer was the first Latino actor to win Best Actor at the Oscars. For which film?
fact: José Ferrer won in 1951 for Cyrano de Bergerac. He was born in Santurce, Puerto Rico.

question: Before switching to acting, what subject did Benicio del Toro originally enroll to study at UC San Diego?
fact: Del Toro enrolled at UC San Diego intending to major in business. An acting class convinced him to change course entirely; he later trained at the Stella Adler Studio in New York.

question: Which Puerto Rican-set film received a Best Picture Oscar nomination in 1962?
fact: West Side Story won 10 Oscars including Best Picture, depicting the Puerto Rican community in NYC.

question: What government agency has supported Puerto Rican film production since the 1950s?
fact: The Puerto Rico Film Commission has attracted over $700M in production spending to the island.

question: Raúl Juliá won a posthumous Golden Globe and Emmy Award for playing which real-life figure in a 1994 film?
fact: In The Burning Season (1994) Juliá portrayed Brazilian environmental activist Chico Mendes. He died in October 1994 before the awards were given — both were posthumous.
